            norm_num [Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_one]
            <;>
              linarith
        -- Use the fact that the norm is non-negative and the integrability of the p-th power
        -- to deduce the integrability of the square.
        have h₁₀ : Integrable (fun ω => ‖X j ω‖ ^ 2) μ := by
          have h₁₁ : (‖X j‖ : Ω → ℝ) ≥ 0 := by
            intro ω
            exact norm_nonneg _
          have h₁₂ : (‖X j‖ : Ω → ℝ) ^ (2 : ℕ) ≤ (‖X j‖ : Ω → ℝ) ^ (2 * m : ℕ) := by
            intro ω
            exact pow_le_pow_right (by
              have h₁₃ : 0 ≤ ‖X j ω‖ := by positivity
              linarith) (by
                cases m with
                | zero => norm_num at *
                | succ m' =>
                  norm_num [Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_one]
                  <;>
                    linarith)
          exact h₇.of_nonneg_of_le (by positivity) (by
            simpa [pow_mul] using h₁₂)
        exact h₁₀
      -- Use the fact that the product of two square-integrable functions is integrable.
      have h₆ : Integrable (fun ω => ‖X i ω‖ * ‖X j ω‖) μ := by
        have h₇ : Integrable (fun ω => ‖X i ω‖ ^ 2) μ := h₄
        have h₈ : Integrable (fun ω => ‖X j ω‖ ^ 2) μ := h₅
        have h₉ : Integrable (fun ω => (‖X i ω‖ * ‖X j ω‖) ^ 2) μ := by
          have h₁₀ : (fun ω : Ω => (‖X i ω‖ * ‖X j ω‖) ^ 2) = (fun ω : Ω => ‖X i ω‖ ^ 2 * ‖X j ω‖ ^ 2) := by
            funext ω
            ring_nf
          rw [h₁₀]
          have h₁₁ : Integrable (fun ω : Ω => ‖X i ω‖ ^ 2 * ‖X j ω‖ ^ 2) μ := by
            -- Use the fact that the product of two square-integrable functions is square-integrable.
            have h₁₂ : Integrable (fun ω => ‖X i ω‖ ^ 2) μ := h₇
            have h₁₃ : Integrable (fun ω => ‖X j ω‖ ^ 2) μ := h₈
            exact Integrable.mul h₁₂ h₁₃
          exact h₁₁
        -- Use the fact that the square of the product is integrable to deduce the integrability of the product itself.
        have h₁₀ : ∀ ω : Ω, 0 ≤ ‖X i ω‖ * ‖X j ω‖ := by
          intro ω
          positivity
        have h₁₁ : Integrable (fun ω => ‖X i ω‖ * ‖X j ω‖) μ := by
          -- Use the fact that the square of the product is integrable to deduce the integrability of the product itself.
          have h₁₂ : Integrable (fun ω => (‖X i ω‖ * ‖X j ω‖) ^ 2) μ := h₉
          have h₁₃ : ∀ ω : Ω, 0 ≤ ‖X i ω‖ * ‖X j ω‖ := h₁₀
          -- Use the fact that the square of the product is integrable to deduce the integrability of the product itself.
          have h₁₄ : Integrable (fun ω => ‖X i ω‖ * ‖X j ω‖) μ := by
            -- Use the fact that the square of the product is integrable to deduce the integrability of the product itself.
            refine' (by
              -- Use the fact that the square of the product is integrable to deduce the integrability of the product itself.
              simpa [sq] using h₁₂.of_nonneg_of_le (fun ω => by positivity) (fun ω => by
                nlinarith [sq_nonneg (‖X i ω‖ * ‖X j ω‖)]))
          exact h₁₄
        exact h₁₁
      exact h₆
    -- Use the fact that the product of the inner products is bounded by the product of the norms.
    have h₄ : Integrable (fun ω ↦ ∏ k : Fin m, inner ℝ (X (I k).1 ω) (X (I k).2 ω)) μ := by
      sorry
    exact h₄
  sorry
sorry
